prompt: isometric pixel-art game tiles for a top-down RPG map. Each tile is a DIFFERENT large object standing FREE on a small flat patch of pale-blue crystalline ice — the object itself rises straight up and IS the sprite. Do NOT put it on a raised block, cube, column, box, crate or pedestal, and do NOT draw the ground as a tall raised cube: just a small low patch at the base, everything else transparent. Go for maximum variety — different shapes, species, ages and conditions, no two tiles alike. The set: 1) a tall spiral ice horn 2) a towering translucent ice pillar 3) a giant glowing crystal spire 4) a tall shard of violet crystal 5) an angular crystal monolith 6) a tall cluster of ice blades 7) a tall frozen fountain of ice 8) an ice-encased ruined tower 9) a glowing crystal obelisk 10) a great geode split with glowing crystals. cozy hand-crafted top-down RPG pixel art, rich natural texture, cohesive harmonious palette, soft directional shading, high quality game asset, transparent background
